plan.title: Echelon Legal — Growth Audit & Plan
plan.subtitle: Site Audit + Local Growth Engagement
plan.audience: For Alexander Karantzalis, Esq. · Echelon Legal, PLLC
plan.author: Evans Diaz · DCF
plan.pitchDateLabel: Pitch delivered Jul 19, 2026 · Meeting held Jul 20
plan.dataNote: This is a real, open pitch — not a hired engagement yet. The audit findings and ranking data are live research from 2026-07-19. The Rhythm, Funnel, Content, and Milestones sections describe the engagement DCF is proposing to run if retained — labelled as the plan, not as results already delivered.

links.label: Echelon Legal, PLLC — the firm this plan is written for

plan.milestonesTitle: The Plan — In the Order That Pays
plan.heroHeadline: Echelon Legal is a 4.6★ firm with a |3-page website.
plan.heroSub: Your reputation is already excellent — but online, Weston can't find you. This is the audit, the ranking reality, and the plan to fix it. All data gathered live on July 19, 2026.

fiveFs.name: Focus
fiveFs.desc: Are we spending the week on the highest-leverage work for this specific firm? Local SEO for a solo estate-planning attorney is not generic content — it is Weston, Broward, and Miami-Dade pages built around his actual practice mix, nothing else.
fiveFs.action: Echelon Legal setup: business, estate & trusts, real estate, and probate get their own pages before any general content work starts. Every page ties back to a practice area he actually bills, not a keyword list.

fiveFs.name: Field
fiveFs.desc: Do we have the right team and stack? This is the same content and local-SEO engine DCF already runs for two other law-firm clients — proven, not improvised.
fiveFs.action: Echelon Legal setup: the same playbook running for Robert Davis P.A. and Gerlach Legal — practice-area page templates, schema.org Attorney markup, Google Business Profile management, review-request automation.

fiveFs.name: Fundamentals
fiveFs.desc: Are we doing the rebuild in the order that actually pays? Platform first, then local visibility, then language coverage, then conversion, then compounding proof — skipping ahead wastes the early weeks.
fiveFs.action: Echelon Legal fundamentals: rebuild the platform (Week 1) before chasing rankings (Week 2) before going bilingual (Week 2) before adding AI intake (Week 3). Reviews and measurement run ongoing underneath all of it.

fiveFs.name: Forecast
fiveFs.desc: Can we predict whether this retainer renews? A live client dashboard tracking rankings, calls, and bookings means neither of us is guessing by month three.
fiveFs.action: Echelon Legal KPIs: Weston/Broward/Miami-Dade ranking position, review count and rating, consult bookings via Calendly and AI intake, and a quarterly re-audit that re-runs this exact report to show movement.

fiveFs.name: Fun
fiveFs.desc: Does the relationship hold? A one-attorney firm has no bandwidth to chase a vendor — the relationship works when the vendor over-communicates instead of waiting to be asked.
fiveFs.action: Echelon Legal culture: a real person answering when he calls, a dashboard he can check without asking, and a build that ships on the stated timeline — Foundation delivers inside one week, not "soon."

rhythmStory: This is the same weekly discipline DCF runs for every retained client: Monday, twenty minutes, same five questions. Focus — which pages ship this week. Field — is the stack (content templates, schema, GBP tools) actually in place. Fundamentals — are we following the build order or skipping ahead. Forecast — what does the live dashboard show. Fun — is he getting real communication, not silence between invoices. For a solo attorney with no marketing staff of his own, that predictability is worth as much as any single tactic.
funnelStory: Right now Echelon Legal's funnel has a hole at the very top: the audit found zero page-one Weston results for his practice areas, against five-plus competitors who do rank. The firm is invisible to anyone who doesn't already know his name — branded searches find him, practice-area searches don't. The proposed funnel below is what DCF would build to close that gap: local visibility first, then a conversion layer that turns the traffic those rankings produce into booked consults. These are the engagement's targets, not measurements of a campaign already running.

funnelStages.math: (Weston · Broward · Miami-Dade dedicated landing pages + GBP optimization)
(business · estate & trusts · real estate · probate — target)
(Calendly + 24/7 AI intake — qualifies, routes, briefs before the call)
(review-request engine compounds the 4.6★ base after each closed matter)

funnelSources.label: Local Search Source
funnelSources.desc: Dedicated Weston, Broward, and Miami-Dade landing pages plus Google Business Profile optimization and directory cleanup (Justia, Yelp, Avvo). Every page-one competitor got there with dedicated, content-rich local pages — that is structure, not luck, and it is exactly what the current 3-page site does not have.

funnelSources.label: Bilingual Source
funnelSources.desc: Broward and Miami-Dade are majority-bilingual markets. Direct competitor RHG Legal already advertises Spanish fluency; Echelon's site is English-only today. A full /es/ version opens a lane a solo-attorney competitor has not covered.

qualificationGate.label: Practice-Area Fit Gate — must match one of four

items.text: state & trusts — wills, estate planning, probate administration.
eal estate — residential and commercial transactions.

qualificationGate.hardRule: Content and landing pages are built practice-area by practice-area, in the order that reflects his actual caseload — not a generic law-firm template.

aeAcceptance.label: Consult-Quality Bar (proposed — tracked once live)

items.text: Local-intent search (city + practice area), practice area matches one of the four, booked via Calendly or AI intake with a clear ask.
Branded search or referral with a general question — good fit, just needs the right practice-area page to land on.
Outside the four practice areas or outside South Florida — AI intake routes these out rather than booking them.

aeAcceptance.target: Target: rankings and bookings tracked on the live client dashboard from day one, so fit is measured, not assumed.

contentWedges.head: Platform Rebuild — Week 1
contentWedges.observation: Today: three pages total — home, thank-you, and a privacy page that still carries a towing company's template text ("Call for 24-hour roadside assistance").
contentWedges.valueLink: An 8–12 page premium rebuild: dedicated business, estate & trusts, real estate, and probate pages, an attorney bio page carrying FL Bar #124755, a review showcase for the 4.6★ rating, and every legal page fixed and schema-marked.
contentWedges.ask: Ships inside one week under the Foundation tier — the deposit locks the build slot.
contentWedges.qualifies: This is the item every other step depends on. Rankings, bilingual pages, and AI intake all need real pages to attach to — this is why it goes first.

contentWedges.head: Own Weston Searches — Week 2
contentWedges.observation: Today: Echelon Legal does not appear anywhere in Weston practice-area search results — only branded searches for the firm's own name find it.
contentWedges.valueLink: Dedicated local landing pages for Weston, Broward, and Miami-Dade, a fully optimized Google Business Profile with per-service descriptions, and directory consistency cleanup across Justia, Yelp, and Avvo.
contentWedges.ask: Directly closes the gap the audit's ranking snapshot shows — every page-one competitor has this structure and Echelon does not, yet.
contentWedges.qualifies: This is the step that actually produces new-client search traffic. Everything downstream (bilingual reach, AI intake, reviews) only matters once this exists.

contentWedges.head: Speak Both Languages — Week 2
contentWedges.observation: Broward and Miami-Dade are majority-bilingual markets, and the current site is English-only.
contentWedges.valueLink: A full Spanish-language version of the site — not a machine-translated afterthought.
contentWedges.ask: Closest competitor RHG Legal already advertises Spanish; near-zero other competitors cover it, which leaves a real lane open.
contentWedges.qualifies: Half the addressable market in these counties, largely uncontested outside one competitor.

contentWedges.head: Convert Around the Clock — Week 3
contentWedges.observation: Estate-planning leads often search at night, after the workday — a phone-and-form-only site misses them.
contentWedges.valueLink: An AI intake assistant that qualifies the visitor, routes them into the existing Calendly flow, and emails Echelon the brief before the call.
contentWedges.ask: Keeps the existing Calendly booking flow intact — this adds a qualification layer in front of it, it doesn't replace it.
contentWedges.qualifies: Converts the after-hours traffic the new ranking pages will start producing, instead of losing it overnight.

contentWhy: This isn't outreach copy — it's the actual build order DCF is proposing, in the order that pays. Same playbook already running for two other law-firm clients (Robert Davis P.A., Gerlach Legal): fix the foundation, then own the neighborhood, then speak to the whole market, then convert what shows up, then compound the proof with reviews and a live dashboard. Nothing here is generic agency filler — every item traces back to a specific gap the July 19 audit found.

items: Ship the premium 8–12 page rebuild — practice-area pages, attorney bio with FL Bar #124755, review showcase, fixed privacy/legal pages, schema.org Attorney markup
Publish dedicated Weston, Broward, and Miami-Dade landing pages
Optimize Google Business Profile with per-service descriptions; clean up Justia/Yelp/Avvo consistency
Ship the full Spanish-language version
Preserve and verify the existing Calendly booking flow through the rebuild
Live client dashboard stood up — rankings, calls, and bookings visible from day one
AI intake assistant live — qualifies visitors, routes to Calendly, briefs Echelon before each call
Post-matter review-request engine running, growing the 4.6★ base
First re-check of Weston/Broward/Miami-Dade practice-area rankings against the July 19 baseline
Directory listings verified consistent across all sources
First full month of dashboard data reviewed together
Quarterly competitive re-audit — this same report, refreshed, run against RHG Legal and the rest of the page-one field
Ranking movement on the four practice-area pages measured against the zero-visibility baseline
Consult-booking volume from AI intake and Calendly reviewed against pre-engagement volume
Retainer renewal conversation grounded in the dashboard, not a guess
Content engine cadence (practice-area articles) established for the next quarter

phaseProof: By day 90, Echelon Legal should have a 12+ page site live, measurable ranking movement on Weston/Broward/Miami-Dade practice-area pages from a zero-visibility baseline, a live Spanish version, a 24/7 intake system, and a quarterly re-audit proving the movement — not a promise, the plan already scoped in the July 19 audit. First mover matters here: the closest competitor's site hasn't been touched since 2024, and nobody else in this market has a modern, conversion-built platform yet.
closingPoints: "Echelon Legal is a 4.6-star firm with a 3-page website." That's the whole problem in one line — the reputation is already earned, the platform just isn't carrying it yet.

"RHG wins on content but their design and booking are years behind." Nobody in this market has a premium, modern, conversion-built site. The first firm that does owns the impression — and with your rating and one-attorney agility, that can be you before Q4.
The plan runs in the order that pays: platform first, then Weston visibility, then bilingual reach, then round-the-clock conversion, then reviews and measurement running underneath all of it.

Three ways in, all three fix every critical item the audit found. Foundation at $1,500 rebuilds the platform in a week. Local Dominance at $3,000 — the recommended tier — adds the three-county visibility and bilingual layer. The Echelon System at $5,000 plus $500 a month adds AI intake, a content engine, and the quarterly re-audit that proves it's working.
Why now: your closest competitor's site hasn't been touched since 2024, and nobody in Weston has a modern platform yet. The deposit locks the build slot; Foundation delivers inside one week.

openQuestions.q: Which concept direction resonates — The Ascent (warm, light, trust-first) or Echelon Rising (dark, modern, confident)?
openQuestions.why: Both concepts were built specifically around the firm's name and story rather than as generic templates. Locking a direction early keeps the one-week Foundation timeline realistic.

openQuestions.q: Which tier fits where the practice is headed — Foundation, Local Dominance, or The Echelon System?
openQuestions.why: Foundation fixes the platform. Local Dominance adds the three-county visibility layer this audit's ranking data says is missing. The Echelon System adds the ongoing growth machine. The right tier depends on whether the goal is a fixed platform or a compounding one.

openQuestions.q: Is there existing content — case outcomes, client stories, background — worth building the new bio and practice-area pages around, rather than starting from a blank page?
openQuestions.why: A rebuild goes faster and reads more credibly when it's built from real material he already has, not generic placeholder copy.

auditFindings.label: echelonlegalfl.com — live audit, 2026-07-19
auditFindings.intro: We audited echelonlegalfl.com the way a prospective client — and Google — actually sees it, the same day this plan was prepared. Two critical issues, three high-priority gaps, one thing already working well.
auditFindings.sourceLine: Source: live crawl of echelonlegalfl.com + Google-indexed pages, 2026-07-19.

findings.title: Privacy page belongs to a towing company
findings.detail: The privacy policy still contains its template's original text: "Call (978) 327-1339 for 24-hour roadside assistance." That is what Google displays when the privacy page appears in results.
findings.fix: Replace with a real, firm-specific privacy policy as part of the platform rebuild.
findings.evidenceSource: Found verbatim on echelonlegalfl.com's privacy policy page — the previous template's text was never replaced. Checked live 2026-07-19.

findings.title: Three pages. Total.
findings.detail: Home, thank-you, privacy. No practice-area pages, no attorney bio, no content. Google has almost nothing to rank, and clients researching "who is this lawyer?" find nothing to build trust on.
findings.fix: 8–12 page rebuild: dedicated pages for business, estate & trusts, real estate, and probate, plus a real bio page.
findings.evidence: Zero of the 8 checked Weston practice-area search results include Echelon Legal. Every competitor on the list has a dedicated local landing page; Echelon ranks for its own name only.
findings.evidenceSource: Localized Weston, FL search snapshot, 2026-07-19 — see the Where You Rank tab for the full result list.

findings.title: The attorney's name isn't on the website
findings.detail: "Alexander Karantzalis" appears nowhere on the homepage — no bio, no photo, no Florida Bar credential (#124755, good standing). People hire lawyers, not logos.
findings.fix: Attorney bio page with name, photo, and Bar credential, built into the rebuild.
findings.evidence: "Alexander Karantzalis" does not appear anywhere on the homepage — no bio, no photo, no Florida Bar #124755 credential.

findings.title: The 4.6★ reputation is invisible
findings.detail: Real reviews exist ("AK is the consummate professional!") — none appear on the site. Every ranked competitor showcases theirs.
findings.fix: Live review showcase with star schema markup, plus a review-request engine to keep it growing.
findings.evidence: "AK is the consummate professional!" — a real client review, live on Google, never displayed anywhere on the firm's own site.
findings.evidenceSource: Google Business Profile reviews cross-checked against echelonlegalfl.com, 2026-07-19.

findings.title: No Spanish version
findings.detail: Broward and Miami-Dade are majority-bilingual markets. Direct competitor RHG Legal advertises Spanish fluency prominently; this site is English-only.
findings.fix: Full /es/ version as part of the Local Dominance tier.
findings.evidence: RHG Legal — the closest same-market competitor — already advertises Spanish fluency prominently. echelonlegalfl.com is English-only, in a county where Spanish is spoken in the majority of households.
findings.evidenceSource: Side-by-side crawl of echelonlegalfl.com vs. RHG Legal, 2026-07-19.

findings.title: Good bones to build on
findings.detail: Calendly booking is wired, the phone call-to-action is clear, and the firm name itself is strong. Nothing here needs rescuing — it needs a real platform underneath it.

rankingTable.label: Who owns "estate planning attorney Weston FL"
rankingTable.note: Live search snapshot, localized to Weston — this is what the next client searching for this practice area actually sees. Echelon Legal appears only when someone already knows the firm's name; branded searches for "Echelon Legal" do find it — this snapshot is for the practice-area search a stranger would run.
rankingTable.sourceLine: Source: localized web search (Weston, FL), 2026-07-19 — snapshot, not a continuous rank tracker.

rows.why: Directory authority — the firm's own profile there is unclaimed and unoptimized.
Dedicated "Weston Estate Planning Attorney" landing page.
Same practice mix, 6-section site, bilingual, testimonials shown.
"25 years in Weston" story plus a dedicated page.
Weston page with a free-consultation CTA.
Not present. Ranks for the firm's own name only.

competitorTable.label: Head-to-head with the closest competitor
competitorTable.note: RHG Legal sits one neighborhood away with the same practice mix. Here's the honest comparison, including where Echelon can leapfrog them.
competitorTable.sourceLine: Source: live crawls of both sites, 2026-07-19 — the "after rebuild" column is proposal scope, not current state.

activityLog.label: Added to the Plan of Action Hub
activityLog.detail: Echelon Legal added as a live consulting example alongside the interview plans — real audit findings, ranking data, and the proposed engagement, all traced back to the July 19 pitch package.

activityLog.label: Pitch meeting held
activityLog.detail: Meeting with Alexander Karantzalis, Esq. to walk through the audit and the three-tier offer.

activityLog.label: Growth audit and pitch package built
activityLog.detail: Live site audit, Weston-area ranking snapshot, RHG Legal competitive comparison, two concept mockups (The Ascent, Echelon Rising), and the three-tier offer — all researched and built same-day.

conceptsIntro: Not templates — concept mockups designed around the firm's own name. "Echelon" means a level, a formation that rises. Both directions make that the story; final art direction gets tuned with the client.

concepts.name: The Ascent
concepts.desc: Warm, established, trust-first. A scroll journey that climbs: build it, protect it, pass it on. Feels like the firm a family hands its legacy to.

concepts.name: Echelon Rising
concepts.desc: Modern, premium, confident. See-through display type over a rising-levels scene. Feels like the firm a founder hires before the big deal.

offerIntro: Three ways in. Every tier fixes the critical audit items — the difference is how far up the echelon you want to climb.

offerTiers.name: Foundation
items: Premium 8-page rebuild (either concept — The Ascent or Echelon Rising)
Practice-area pages + attorney bio & Florida Bar #124755 credential
Review showcase — the 4.6★ rating, finally visible on-site
Privacy & legal pages rebuilt — the roadside-assistance boilerplate gone day one
Calendly integration preserved

offerTiers.name: Local Dominance
items: Everything in Foundation (12+ pages)
Weston / Broward / Miami-Dade landing pages
Google Business Profile optimization
Directory cleanup & consistency (Justia, Yelp, Avvo)
Full Spanish version (/es/)
Review-request engine
Monthly growth retainer

offerTiers.name: The Echelon System
items: Everything in Local Dominance
AI intake assistant (24/7 qualify, book, brief)
Monthly content engine (practice-area articles)
Live client dashboard — rankings, calls, bookings
Quarterly competitive re-audit (this document, refreshed)
Priority support & iteration

offerWhyNow: The closest competitor's site hasn't been touched since 2024, and nobody in Weston has a modern platform yet. First mover owns the impression. The deposit locks the build slot; Foundation delivers inside one week.
